vulnerabilities.description: HTML comment discloses a test account (test:test). Using these credentials logs in successfully and sets session/JWT cookies.
Submitting an existing username returns a personalized password page (200 OK), while non-existent usernames redirect with an error. This allows enumeration of valid usernames.
Password page exposes internal user_id in a hidden field and greets the user by name before full authentication, leaking PII and internal identifiers.
JWT auth_token cookie is set without HttpOnly or Secure flags, making it accessible to client-side scripts and susceptible to theft if XSS is present.
Logout form uses POST without any visible CSRF token or protection, allowing cross-site request forgery to trigger state-changing actions.
Server header reveals Apache/2.4.66 (Debian), disclosing software and version information.
